statistics.name: assists
statistics.description: The number of times a player who passes the ball to a teammate in a way that leads to a score by field goal, meaning that he or she was "assisting" in the basket. There is some judgment involved in deciding whether a passer should be credited with an assist.

articles.headline: Steve Kerr, Doc Rivers join 'political interference' letter
articles.description: A number of prominent basketball coaches, including Steve Kerr and Doc Rivers, signed a public letter released Wednesday contending that political interference in universities threatens to undermine college athletics.

articles.headline: How every five-star men's college basketball commit fits at his new school
articles.description: Breaking down top class of 2026 commitments, including a third five-star recruit for BYU in three years.

images.name: Bruce Branch III [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruce Branch III, the former No. 2 prospect who reclassified up from the 2027 class, has committed to BYU, giving Kevin Young a third consecutive class featuring a five-star recruit.

images.name: Jordan Smith [1296x729]
images.caption: Jordan Smith is the second five-star recruit of the 2026 high school class to commit to Arkansas.

images.name: Cameron Williams commit fit
images.caption: A top-3 recruit in the class of 2026, Cameron Williams is headed to Durham to play for Jon Scheyer and the Duke Blue Devils.

images.name: Jason Crowe [1144x644]
images.caption: Jason Crowe, No. 4 recruit in the 2026 ESPN 100 player rankings, has committed to Missouri.

images.name: Babatunde Oladotun [1066x600]
images.caption: Babatunde Oladotun, the former 2027 No. 1 prospect who reclassified to the 2026 class and is ranked in the top-10, has announced his commitment to Maryland.

images.name: Dylan Mingo [1296x729]
images.caption: Premier class of 2026 guard Dylan Mingo has committed to North Carolina for the 2026-27 season.

images.name: Caleb Gaskins [1296x729]
images.caption: Caleb Gaskins is staying in-state next season to play for Miami and first-time head coach Jai Lucas.

images.name: Deron Rippey Jr. [1296x729]
images.caption: Deron Rippey Jr. has committed to Duke, giving the Blue Devils another top recruit.

images.name: Toni Bryant (commitment photo) [600x338]
images.caption: Toni Bryant, the No. 14 recruit in the class of 2026, has committed to Missouri, giving the Tigers two five-star prospects for the 2026-27 season.

images.name: Austin Goosby (Texas) [1296x729]
images.caption: Austin Goosby has committed to Sean Miller and Texas for the 2026-27 season.

images.name: Taylen Kinney [1296x729]
images.caption: Taylen Kinney is one of the best point guards in the boys' SC Next class of 2026, and recently committed to Kansas.
images.credit: Brian Rothmuller/Icon Sportswire via Getty Images

article.headline: No. 19 Miami of Ohio beats Toledo 74-72, extends season-opening winning streak to 30 games
article.description: — Pete Suder scored 19 points, Antowne Woolfolk added 14 and No. 19 Miami (Ohio) closed within a victory of a perfect regular season with a 74-72 win over Toledo on Tuesday night that extended the RedHawks' season-opening winning streak to 30 games.
article.linkText: No. 19 Miami of Ohio beats Toledo 74-72, extends season-opening winning streak to 30 games

images.name: Toledo Rockets vs. Miami (OH) Redhawks: Game Highlights
images.caption: Toledo Rockets vs. Miami (OH) Redhawks: Game Highlights

video.description: Toledo Rockets vs. Miami (OH) Redhawks: Game Highlights
video.shortDescription: Toledo Rockets vs. Miami (OH) Redhawks: Game Highlights

tracking.trackingName: NCB_One-Play (Toledo Rockets vs. Miami (OH) Redhawks: Game Highlights) 2026/03/04 ESHEET

article.story: OXFORD, Ohio -- — Pete Suder scored 19 points, Antowne Woolfolk added 14 and No. 19 Miami (Ohio) closed within a victory of a perfect regular season with a 74-72 win over Toledo on Tuesday night that extended the RedHawks' season-opening winning streak to 30 games. The RedHawks (17-0 Mid-American Conference) remain the only undefeated team in Division I men’s basketball. Miami extended the best start in program history and added to its school record for wins in a season. Miami also boasts the best start and longest win streak in MAC annals. Brant Byers added 13 points and Luke Skaljac chipped in 12 for the RedHawks, who extended their home winning streak to 31- games in front of a sellout crowd of 10,640 at Millett Hall. That home mark matches Duke for the longest in the nation. Leroy Blyden Jr. led Toledo (16-4, 10-7) with 21 points and Sonny Wilson added 13. Miami was coming off a 69-67 win over Western Kentucky on Friday night, when they needed a buzzer beater from freshman Trey Perry. In this game, the RedHawks jumped out to an early double-digit lead against the Rockets (16-14, 10-7) and never trailed. Toledo cut the deficit to one on four occasions and looked as though it would take the lead on an Austin Parks drive to the basket with nine minutes remaining, but Miami’s Eian Elmer stuffed him at the rim and hit a 3-pointer a few seconds later to push the lead back to four points. The Rockets trailed by two point and had the ball with 13 seconds remaining, but but turned the ball over with less than a second to play. The victory gave the RedHawks their first season sweep of Toledo since the 1996-97 season, and it ran their series winning streak to three in a row overall after losing 22 straight from 2012-24. Up next Miami: At Ohio University on Friday. Toledo: Hosts Buffalo on Friday. ------ Get poll alerts and updates on the AP Top 25 throughout the season. Sign up here. AP college basketball: https://apnews.com/hub/ap-top-25-college-basketball-poll and https://apnews.com/hub/college-basketball
